summary: Added Examples dimension to rubric scorer. Created prompts/examples.md with verbatim rubric levels, per-platform scoring instruction, and v2 doc-as-test-augmentation note. Scored all 10 pilot pages. avg=4.3, dist 4:7/5:3 — best dimension so far. All 10 pages pass doc-as-test compilation (confirmed before scoring). Score-5 pages: send-a-message, message-flagging, authentication. Score-4 pages: 7 pages held back by missing import statements and/or inconsistent error handling coverage across platforms.

3_lowest_scored_pages.rationale: Error handling present on Swift only; Android/Flutter/TS examples omit failure paths. Also missing imports in Android/Kotlin.
iOS/Android observe closures reference error param without handling it; error handling relegated to a collapsed accordion rather than shown inline.
Realistic upload/download examples but NO import statements in any platform snippet — least extractable of the set.

notes.v1_scoring_adequacy: Single-page judgment is sufficient for Examples. The discriminating factor between 4 and 5 is error handling presence (clearly visible in the page) and import statements (also visible). v2 doc-as-test augmentation would be useful for confirming the 3 score-4 pages truly compile cleanly — but given all 10 passed doc-as-test prior to scoring, the confidence is already high. v2 would mainly help for future pages not yet in the pilot cohort.
notes.dominant_finding: Missing import statements are the most common barrier to score 5. 7 of 10 pages would reach score 5 with imports + consistent cross-platform error handling. The fix is mechanical — 30 min of work per page.
